themes.name: Agentic AI in research
themes.description: Focuses on AI agents that can perform research tasks autonomously, including data gathering, analysis, and workflow management.

themes.name: AI coding assistants
themes.description: Covers AI tools that assist developers in writing, debugging, and reviewing code, including AI-generated code and security analysis of AI-assisted development.

themes.name: AI research tools
themes.description: Encompasses a broad range of AI applications and platforms designed to aid in various research processes, from literature reviews to data analysis.

themes.name: AI for design & research
themes.description: Highlights the use of AI in both the design process and user research, including UI generation, design system automation, and design trend analysis.

themes.name: AI summarization
themes.description: Covers AI tools and techniques for condensing large amounts of text, such as meeting transcripts, research papers, and articles, into concise summaries.

themes.name: AI for product management
themes.description: Explores how AI is being applied to product management tasks, including strategy, conflict identification, and product development workflows.

themes.name: AI chatbot research
themes.description: Focuses on the research and development of AI chatbots, including their applications in shopping, mental health, and security.

themes.name: AI sentiment feedback
themes.description: Deals with AI's role in analyzing customer sentiment and feedback across various channels like reviews, social media, and customer interactions.

themes.name: AI recruitment research
themes.description: Examines how AI is impacting the job market, including studies on job displacement, new hiring trends, and the safety of certain professions from AI automation.

themes.name: AI tool evaluation
themes.description: Focuses on comparing and reviewing different AI tools and platforms for specific use cases and industries.

note: Themes identified by analyzing articles from curated sources, Google News, HackerNews, and DEV.to this month.
